dial.summary: Net macro score +2 (4 supportive: Global M2 $102.4T on continued 4-week uptrend per StreetStats May 2026 aggregate—June data not yet available due to 6–8 week lag, Fed balance sheet $6.74T flat per H.4.1 week ended ~July 17 with next release July 24, BTC funding +0.009%/8h near-zero per KFRI 10.17% annualized as of July 13, stablecoin dominance ~13.1% on continued 30-day descent from ~14.4% mid-June; 2 neutral: ISM Manufacturing 53.3% above-50-but-falling with July data due August 3, 2s10s +0.39% positive-flat on 4-week basis; 2 headwinds: DXY 100.73 marginally above the 100 threshold on flat 4-week trend—weakened only ~0.37% over 30 days, real yields 2.32% elevated and up +0.14pp from ~2.18% on June 17); BTC.D 56.3% firm at $65,094 / $1.31T market cap, TOTAL ~$2.33T; the July 28–29 FOMC hold at 3.5–3.75%—now one week away—is the primary catalyst to compress DXY below 100 and real yields toward 2%, which would flip both remaining headwinds and reinforce BTC Accumulation into Q3; July 30 Q2 GDP advance/June PCE and BOE/BOJ decisions are the secondary macro tests.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: StreetStats 4-bank aggregate (Fed/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) confirmed at $102,442B ($102.4T) as of May 2026—unchanged from prior run; 6–8 week reporting lag means June 2026 aggregate data will not be available until mid-to-late August 2026; 4-week trend up: global M2 has been rising for 3+ consecutive months per StreetStats series, with YoY growth at multi-year high per Mises Institute analysis; US M2 rose ~$118B/month through April 2026 per TradingEconomics, consistent with a rising global aggregate; status supportive (4-week uptrend confirmed; YoY growth positive; contracting YoY condition for headwind not met); next aggregate update expected mid-to-late August 2026 for June 2026 base data.

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive month of expansion, 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; no July 2026 data available—next ISM Manufacturing PMI releases Monday August 3, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET (13 days from today); 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); sub-indicators: new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (contracting), prices paid 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires above 50 AND rising; headwind requires below 48 and falling; current reading satisfies neither extreme condition).

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY closed at 100.7250 on July 20, 2026 (per TradingEconomics July 20 close); intraday July 21 trading near 100.7–100.8, rebounding from a one-month low of 100.5 on July 16; updated from prior run's 100.72 (intraday July 20); 4-week trend flat: DXY was approximately 101.1 on June 17–20 (post-FOMC) vs 100.73 today—a decline of ~0.37%, within the <0.5% relative flat threshold; FRED DTWEXBGS broad dollar API returned 403 via proxy this run—trend derived from ICE DXY spot data (the two series correlate closely); status headwind (ICE DXY at 100.73 remains above the 100 threshold; the July 28–29 FOMC hold with Chair Warsh dovish language on disinflation is the primary near-term catalyst for a sustained close below 100).

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) at 2.32% as of July 18–21, 2026 (MacroRadar and TradingEconomics confirm 2.32%; value unchanged from prior run); FRED API returned 403 via proxy—value sourced from web search aggregates; 4-week trend up: DFII10 rose approximately +0.14pp from ~2.18% on June 17 to 2.32%, well above the 5bp flat threshold; June CPI at 3.5% YoY and soft June PPI are compressing nominal 10Y yields (4.60% on July 20), but breakevens moved in tandem limiting real yield compression; status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis—a sustained move to or below 2.00% required to flip to neutral; July 30 Q2 GDP advance and June PCE are the next significant disinflation tests).

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at 4.60% and 2-year at 4.21% as of July 20, 2026 (TradingEconomics: 10Y rose to 4.60% on July 20; 2Y rose to 4.21% on July 20); spread: 4.60% − 4.21% = +0.39%; updated from prior run's +0.37% (July 17: 10Y 4.55%, 2Y 4.18%); 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.39% on June 17 (post-FOMC), now +0.39%—a 0bp change on the 4-week basis, well within the flat threshold; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the −50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold; not steepening on a 30-day basis—supportive requires positive AND steepening; July 28–29 FOMC hold with dovish guidance is the primary near-term catalyst for steepening).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: Most recent H.4.1: week ended ~July 16–17, 2026 at approximately $6,743B ($6.74T) per FRED search aggregates; FRED search also confirms $6,735,609M ($6.736T) for week ended July 9 (H.4.1 dated July 9); FRED API returned 403 via proxy—value sourced from web search; next H.4.1 release Thursday July 24, 2026 for week ending July 23; 4-week comparison: week ended ~June 18 estimated ~$6.71T; 4-week change ~+$25–30B / ~+0.4%, within the flat threshold; status supportive (balance sheet flat with no acceleration of QT; year-over-year growth modestly positive at ~+0.4% per FRED search; no aggressive reserve drain).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: CF Bitcoin Kraken Perpetual Funding Rate Index (KFRI, cfbenchmarks.com) showed 10.17% annualized as of July 13, 2026—equivalent to approximately +0.0093% per 8-hour settlement (10.17% ÷ 3 settlements/day ÷ 365 days); updated from prior run's +0.01% per 8h (≈10.95% annualized); CoinGlass, coinalyze, and Bitcoin Magazine Pro again returned HTTP 403 this run; 4-week trend flat: the July 13 reading is near-zero and broadly consistent with the prior +0.01% reading—no material directional shift; status supportive (well below the 0.04% overheating threshold; near-zero funding during stable-to-rising TOTAL market cap is consistent with spot-driven BTC Accumulation dynamics).

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap estimated ~$295–305B against total crypto market cap ~$2.33T as of July 21, 2026 (multiple sources: July 12–13 data showed $290.8–303.2B across trackers; StableCoin.com live: ~$290B; BTC.D at 56.3%, BTC price $65,094 / market cap $1.31T per Coinbase/Binance, implied TOTAL = $1.31T ÷ 0.563 = ~$2.33T; CoinGecko and DefiLlama returned HTTP 403 this run); stablecoin dominance estimated ~13.1% (data range: 12.6–13.3% depending on source); 30-day trend down: dominance was ~14.4% mid-June, 13.34% on July 20 (prior run), and ~13.1% today—a ~1.3pp decline over 30 days; declining stablecoin dominance alongside stable-to-rising TOTAL indicates capital staying in crypto rather than rotating to cash; status supportive (30-day downtrend intact; headwind condition requires rising stablecoin dominance while TOTAL also rises—not met as TOTAL is stable-to-rising).

upcoming.title: Existing Home Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Realtors (NAR) Existing Home Sales for June 2026—closings on previously owned homes reflecting mortgage lock-in dynamics in a still-elevated rate environment (10Y ~4.60%, TIPS real yield 2.32%); released during FOMC blackout (July 18–30); one trading day before the ECB rate decision on July 23, which will set cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: ECB Rate Decision (July 2026)
upcoming.note: ECB Governing Council decision at 13:45 CET (7:45 AM ET), press conference at 14:30 CET (8:30 AM ET)—July is a non-projection meeting (no updated Staff Economic Projections); ECB deposit facility rate at 2.25% (raised 25bps at the June 11 meeting); markets price a high probability of a hold at July 23; Lagarde's press conference tone is the primary market driver; will assess whether global disinflation (US CPI 3.5% YoY, soft US PPI) and commodity easing reduce urgency for further action; ECB guidance sets cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 19, 2026)
upcoming.note: Weekly UI claims at 8:30 AM ET—same morning as ECB decision; last jobless claims print before the July 28–29 FOMC rate decision (announced Wednesday July 29 at 2:00 PM ET); two consecutive weeks above 240K would materially shift rate-cut pricing entering the Wednesday announcement; the ECB + claims combination on July 23 is the most consequential macro morning before the FOMC meeting begins July 28.

upcoming.title: S&P Global Flash US PMI / New Home Sales (July 2026)
upcoming.note: S&P Global Preliminary (Flash) US Manufacturing and Services PMI for July 2026 at ~9:45 AM ET—first comprehensive business activity read for Q3, released four days before the July 28–29 FOMC; a manufacturing flash PMI above 53 consistent with the June ISM (53.3%) would signal expansion broadening into Q3; services PMI is watched for sticky wage-driven services inflation; Census Bureau New Home Sales (June 2026) also at 10:00 AM ET; among the last major indicators the Committee will assess before the FOMC meeting begins July 28.

upcoming.title: Advance Durable Goods Orders (June 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau Advance Monthly Durable Goods for June 2026 at 8:30 AM ET—core capital goods orders (ex-defense, ex-aircraft) are the key business investment sub-component; last significant growth indicator before the two-day FOMC meeting begins July 28; a solid capex print alongside the soft CPI/PPI backdrop would reinforce the soft-landing case for a hold at 3.5–3.75%; weak orders could add downside risk to the Committee's growth outlook.

upcoming.title: FOMC Meeting Day 1 (July 28–29, 2026)
upcoming.note: Two-day FOMC meeting begins July 28; rate decision and Chair Warsh press conference on July 29 at 2:00 PM ET / 2:30 PM ET—non-SEP meeting (no dot plot or updated economic projections); current target range: 3.5–3.75%; data the Committee will assess: June CPI (3.5% YoY, July 14), soft June PPI (July 15), June retail sales (July 16), July Flash PMI (July 24), June durable goods (July 27); consensus strongly favors a hold at 3.5–3.75%.
